per_question.question_text: Can you explain the difference between a LEFT JOIN and an INNER JOIN in SQL, and give me a simple business scenario where you'd choose one over the other?
per_question.answer_text: तो जो winner join है वो सिर्फ returns only the records that have matching values in both Tables. और जो left join है that returns all records from the left Left table and matching records from the Right table. If there is no match नल वैल्यूज आर रिटर्न For the right table column. Suppose we have customer table and order table. If I want to see only customer who Placed order

per_question.fix: Practice explaining technical concepts in English to improve clarity and concision

per_question.question_text: Your resume mentions your work at KAS Marine contributed to a 5% improvement in fuel efficiency recommendations. Can you walk me through exactly how you analyzed the vessel performance data and what specific trend you found that led to this recommendation?
per_question.answer_text: तो जब मैं internship कर रहा था उस वक्त I analyze Vessel Operations Data Including Voyage Duration, Speed Pay Fuel consumption and route details. I first clean the data set by removing duplicates, handling missing values and standardizing units. Power भी है और Excel का यूज़ करके। उसके बाद मैं dashboard create करूंगा to analyze fuel consumption across routes and vessel types. I discovered that certain vessel operation above optimal speed constantly Consume Disproportional Higher Field. व highlighting this trend and recommending maintaining speed within an optimal range The team was able to improve फ्यूल एफिशिएंसी by approximately 5%.
per_question.develop: Quantifying the impact of speed on fuel consumption and exploring additional factors
per_question.fix: Consider using data visualization tools like Power BI to create interactive dashboards and further analyze vessel performance metrics

per_question.question_text: Our JD and your resume both mention SQL window functions. Can you tell me about a time you used a window function, like RANK() or LAG(), to answer a specific business question that would have been difficult to solve otherwise?
per_question.answer_text: My approach would be verify whether the drop Drop is real. channel और Cost by data issues check Check ETL pipelines and source system, compare current data and historical trends. Segment data by channel validates those data building stakeholder trust comes from transparency and consistent Accuracy using a join year would be necessary complex sub queries make such Compression simpler and more reliable. अ subquery is useful when the result of one query Is needed to filter Another query. Total Revenue by Roots. This function helps business summarize large data set into meaningful KPS. A report consists of multiple pages which With interactive visuals. अ dashboard is a single page overview created by pinning visuals. From one or more reports. Reports are used for detail analyze while dashboard provide high level Keep ये है।

per_question.fix: focus on clearly explaining a specific scenario where a window function was used to solve a business problem, avoiding vague statements and filler words

per_question.question_text: In Excel, what's the purpose of a Pivot Table, and can you walk me through the basic steps to create one from a raw dataset?
per_question.answer_text: इवेंट टेबल help करता है to summarize, analyze and explore large data sets quickly. विदाउट writing formulas मतलब आपको formulas लिखने की कोई जरूरत नहीं पड़ती। For example if I have sales data containing date Product Region Revenue I can instantly see Total Sales by Region और Monthly Funds. तो जो symbol steps है उसको create करने के वह है select the data set Go to insert Pivot Tables Choose the data range and location. Drag fields into rows, column, values, filters, Customize aggregation such as sum, count or average. This allows quick business analysis with minimal efforts.
per_question.develop: provide more specific examples from personal experience
per_question.fix: practice explaining technical concepts in English for better clarity
